name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
evidence: Photos are stored as BLOBs in Oracle DB and served by database ID. No user-controlled input is resolved to a filesystem path.

name: Relative Path Traversal
evidence: No relative path resolution against user input.

name: Absolute Path Traversal
evidence: No absolute filesystem path is constructed from user input.

name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
evidence: No Runtime.exec(), ProcessBuilder, or OS command construction found.

name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
evidence: No OS command execution APIs found in the codebase.

name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
evidence: Thymeleaf templates exclusively use th:text (HTML-auto-escaped). No th:utext or unescaped raw output expressions detected.

name: Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')
evidence: No command argument construction from user input found.

name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
evidence: All repository queries use Spring Data JPA @Query with @Param named parameters — fully parameterized, no string concatenation.

name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
evidence: No LDAP libraries or patterns present in the codebase.

name: XML Injection (aka Blind XPath Injection)
evidence: No XML parsing or construction from user-controlled input found.

name: Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
evidence: MIME types validated against a whitelist; photo IDs are UUIDs used only as JPA primary keys.

name: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
evidence: No C/C++/Assembly code. Memory buffer operations managed by the JVM.

name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java does not support manual buffer copy without bounds checking.

name: Write-what-where Condition
evidence: No C/C++ code. Arbitrary memory writes are not possible in a managed Java runtime.

name: Out-of-bounds Read
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java enforces array bounds at runtime, preventing out-of-bounds reads.

name: Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
evidence: No length parameter inconsistency patterns found.

name: Use of Hard-coded Password
evidence:
src/main/resources/application.properties (line 12): spring.datasource.password=photoalbum
src/main/resources/application-docker.properties (line 4): spring.datasource.password=photoalbum

name: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
evidence: No cryptographic keys found hard-coded in the codebase.

name: Double Free
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java uses garbage collection; there is no manual free() operation.

name: Use After Free
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java GC prevents use-after-free by managing object lifecycle automatically.

name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java line 83 — file type validation relies solely on the client-provided Content-Type header (file.getContentType()), which an attacker can set to any allowed value while uploading a file of any dangerous type. No magic-byte / server-side content inspection is performed.

name: Missing Initialization of a Variable
evidence: No missing initialization patterns found.

name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
evidence: No uninitialized variable usage patterns found.

name: Use of Obsolete Function
evidence: No obsolete function usage found.

name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
evidence: No ObjectInputStream, readObject(), XMLDecoder, or any Java deserialization mechanism found.

name: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
evidence: No singleton pattern exists — the app uses Spring DI (@Service, @Transactional), not manually managed singletons.

name: SQL Injection: Hibernate
evidence: All repository queries use Spring Data JPA @Query with @Param named parameters — fully parameterized.

name: Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context
evidence: The only static fields are static final Logger instances (immutable). No mutable shared static state exists.

name: Expression is Always False
evidence: No always-false expression patterns found.

name: Expression is Always True
evidence: No always-true expression patterns found.

name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
evidence: HomeController.java:68 — for (MultipartFile file : files) iterates over a user-supplied list (@RequestParam("files") List<MultipartFile> files) with no upper-bound check on the number of files. Only a null/empty guard exists, so an attacker can submit arbitrarily many files and cause excessive looping / resource exhaustion.

name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
evidence: No XML parsing APIs (DocumentBuilder, SAXParser, XMLInputFactory, etc.) present anywhere in the codebase.

name: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
evidence: No XPath libraries or patterns present in the codebase.

name: Improper Neutralization of Data within XQuery Expressions ('XQuery Injection')
evidence: No XQuery libraries or patterns present in the codebase.

name: Improper Synchronization
evidence: No explicit thread management or concurrent resource sharing. Spring transaction management handles data access safely.

name: Improper Initialization
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:115 — byte[] photoData = null; is initialized to null. If file.getBytes() throws a non-IOException RuntimeException, it is silently swallowed by catch (Exception ex) with only a warning log, and execution continues with photoData still null.

name: Improper Locking
evidence: No Lock, ReentrantLock, synchronized blocks, or any locking primitives are used.

name: Operation on a Resource after Expiration or Release
evidence: Resources such as ByteArrayInputStream are properly managed via try-with-resources.

name: Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types
evidence: No incorrect numeric type conversion patterns found.

name: Incorrect Calculation
evidence: No incorrect calculation patterns found.

name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
evidence: No incorrect permission assignment patterns found.

name: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
evidence: Resources are properly managed with try-with-resources throughout the codebase.

name: Missing Release of File Descriptor or Handle after Effective Lifetime
evidence: No file descriptor or handle leaks found.

name: Insufficient Logging
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java (~line 99): The empty-file validation branch (file.getSize() <= 0) sets an error result and returns with no log statement. Additionally, the application has no authentication layer, so there is zero logging of authentication/authorization events.

name: Operator Precedence Logic Error
evidence: No operator precedence logic errors found.

name: Access of Memory Location Before Start of Buffer
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java prevents access before buffer start via runtime bounds checks.

name: Out-of-bounds Write
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java prevents out-of-bounds writes via runtime array bounds enforcement.

name: Access of Memory Location After End of Buffer
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java prevents access past the end of a buffer via ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.

name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value
evidence: No excessive memory allocation patterns found.

name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
evidence:
src/main/resources/application.properties (lines 11-12): spring.datasource.username=photoalbum / spring.datasource.password=photoalbum
src/main/resources/application-docker.properties (lines 3-4): same credentials hard-coded in the Docker profile

name: Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java does not allow manual length-based buffer access that could escape bounds.

name: Missing Synchronization
evidence: All Spring beans hold only final injected fields — no shared mutable state accessed across threads.

name: Incorrect Synchronization
evidence: No synchronization constructs are present to assess for correctness.

name: Untrusted Pointer Dereference
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java does not support raw pointer dereferencing.

name: Use of Out-of-range Pointer Offset
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java does not support pointer arithmetic.

name: Access of Uninitialized Pointer
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java references are always initialized to null before use.

name: Expired Pointer Dereference
evidence: No C/C++ code. Java GC ensures dereferenced objects remain valid as long as a reference is held.

name: Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')
evidence: No infinite loop patterns found.

name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component
evidence: Data access is properly layered through Spring Data JPA repositories.
